ucs.title: [LLM] RedEcho / ShadowPad beaconing: long-lived TLS to dynamically-resolved domains over non-standard ports
ucs.description: Hunts the characteristic ShadowPad C2 pattern attributed to RedEcho (PRC, G1042) — outbound encrypted sessions from internal hosts to attacker-acquired domains resolved via DDNS providers, on non-standard TLS ports (e.g. 443 swapped to 13xxx/14xxx/8443/9443), with low-volume periodic beaconing observed in the Indian power-sector intrusions documented by Recorded Future.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as dest_ips values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_ports values(All_Traffic.app) as app from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port IN (8443,9443,10443,13567,13568,14001,14002,17443,18443) AND All_Traffic.action=allowed AND All_Traffic.bytes_out>0 by All_Traffic.src_ip All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | eventstats dc(_time) as beacon_hours by src_ip dest dest_port | where beacon_hours>=6 AND count<200 | rename dest as c2_host | join type=left c2_host [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.url=* by Web.dest Web.url | `drop_dm_object_name(Web)` | rex field=dest "(?<c2_host>[^/]+)" | stats values(url) as urls by c2_host] | lookup ddns_providers domain as c2_host OUTPUT is_ddns | where is_ddns="true" OR match(c2_host,"\.(ddns|hopto|no-ip|dynu|3322|oray|gnway|chinanetcenter)\.")  | table firstTime lastTime src_ip c2_host dest_ports beacon_hours count app
ucs.kql:
let ddns_regex = @'\.(ddns\.net|hopto\.org|no-ip\.|dynu\.|3322\.org|oray\.com|gnway\.|chickenkiller\.com|crabdance\.com|jumpingcrab\.com)$';
let susPorts = dynamic([8443,9443,10443,13567,13568,14001,14002,17443,18443]);
let beacons = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
| where RemotePort in (susPorts)
| where isnotempty(RemoteUrl) or isnotempty(RemoteIP)
| extend host = tolower(coalesce(RemoteUrl, tostring(RemoteIP)))
| summarize hits = count(),
            firstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            lastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            beaconHours = dcount(bin(Timestamp, 1h)),
            ports = make_set(RemotePort, 10),
            procs = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 10)
        by DeviceId, DeviceName, host
| where beaconHours >= 6 and hits between (12 .. 400);
beacons
| where host matches regex ddns_regex
   or host has_any("ddns","hopto","no-ip","dynu","oray","3322")
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","svchost.exe","mscorsvw.exe")
        or FileName in~ ("hpqhvsei.exe","hpqhvind.exe","oleview.exe")  // ShadowPad-known sideload hosts
    | summarize sideloadEvidence = make_set(strcat(InitiatingProcessFileName,"->",FileName), 5) by DeviceId
) on DeviceId
| project firstSeen, lastSeen, DeviceName, host, ports, beaconHours, hits, procs, sideloadEvidence
| order by beaconHours desc
ucs.rationale: RedEcho's defining signature in the AXIOMATICASYMPTOTE/ShadowPad cluster is overlap of (a) DDNS-resolved attacker domains (T1583.001 + T1568) and (b) TLS C2 deliberately moved off 443 onto sticky non-standard ports (T1571) — Recorded Future explicitly enumerated 8443/9443/13xxx as RedEcho ports against Indian RDSO/POSOCO. Combining DDNS host + non-standard-port + multi-day low-volume beacon + Shad

ucs.title: [LLM] RedEcho ShadowPad DLL search-order hijack via signed third-party loaders
ucs.description: Catches the install-stage tradecraft RedEcho repeatedly uses to land ShadowPad on critical-infrastructure hosts: dropping a malicious DLL alongside a benign signed binary (HP, McAfee, Microsoft, Kaspersky utilities) so the legitimate process side-loads the implant, which then beacons to the DDNS infrastructure flagged in UC1.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("hpqhvsei.exe","hpqhvind.exe","oleview.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","AvastSvc.exe","TmDbgLog.exe","BDReinit.exe") by host Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=left process_guid [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_path) as dropped_dll values(Filesystem.file_name) as dll_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created AND Filesystem.file_name=*.dll AND NOT (Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Windows\\System32\\*","*\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*","*\\Program Files*")) by Filesystem.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename process_guid as process_guid] | where isnotnull(dropped_dll) | join type=left host [| tstats summariesonly=true values(All_Traffic.dest) as c2 values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as c2_port from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port IN (8443,9443,13567,14001) by All_Traffic.src as host | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`] | where isnotnull(c2) | table firstTime host process_name process_path dll_name parent c2 c2_port
ucs.kql:
let sideloadHosts = dynamic(["hpqhvsei.exe","hpqhvind.exe","oleview.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","avastsvc.exe","tmdbglog.exe","bdreinit.exe","log.exe"]);
let susDirs = dynamic([@"\programdata\",@"\public\",@"\perflogs\",@"\appdata\local\temp\",@"\users\public\"]);
let susPorts = dynamic([8443,9443,10443,13567,13568,14001]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (sideloadHosts)
| extend dllDir = tolower(tostring(parse_path(FolderPath).DirectoryName))
| where not(FolderPath has_any (@"\windows\system32\", @"\windows\syswow64\", @"\program files"))
| where dllDir has_any (susDirs) or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (susDirs)
| extend hijack = strcat(InitiatingProcessFileName, " <- ", FileName)
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess" and RemotePort in (susPorts)
    | summarize c2hosts = make_set(RemoteUrl, 10), c2ports = make_set(RemotePort, 5) by DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId, InitiatingProcessCreationTime
) on DeviceId, $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.InitiatingProcessId, $left.InitiatingProcessCreationTime == $right.InitiatingProcessCreationTime
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, hijack, FolderPath, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, c2hosts, c2ports
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: ShadowPad — the implant Recorded Future used to cluster RedEcho across the Indian power-grid intrusions — is delivered almost exclusively via DLL side-loading off signed third-party utilities (notably HP `hpqhvsei.exe` / `hpqhvind.exe` and the `log.dll` loader chain). Pivoting from a known sideload-host process loading an unsigned DLL out of a non-standard directory AND that same process tuple mak
